The statistical analysis reveals a high probability that Version B outperforms Version A in average interaction time due to its lower mean and smaller standard deviation when sampled. This file demonstrates how data drives decision making with minor adjustments yielding significant results for the red team.

Using a quadratic model where variance equals the square of theta times a constant allows us to solve for the specific design parameter needed. Imagination guides the engineer to find theta equals four when reducing variance from twenty five to sixteen while keeping the mean constant.
